# MASTER VISUAL ASSET INVENTORY
## "The Story Behind the Equation" — Physics Education Channel
## Full 22-Topic / 150-Episode Series

*Generated by scanning all 150 YouTube Long scripts across all 22 topics.*
*Last updated: 2026-03-30*

---

## SCOPE AND METHOD

All 150 `ep*_youtube_long.md` scripts were scanned across all 22 topic directories.
Classical Mechanics scripts (topics 01–08) contain explicit `[Visual: ...]` cue tags (506 total tagged).
Electromagnetism, Thermodynamics, and Quantum Mechanics scripts are narrative-only;
visual elements were extracted from section headings, narrative content, and physics concepts described.

**Art style for all illustrated assets:**
Board-book flat illustration — rounded chibi characters, big heads, bold flat colors,
warm pastels, minimal detail, bold outlines, white or solid-color backgrounds.
Reference: *My First Heroes: Inventors* (Sanmin Books) cover aesthetic.
Objects: 1–3 shape masses, no faces, no text, no gradients, no gloss.
Approved reference characters on file: `data/assets/physics/test_mflux/char_newton_v2.png`,
`data/assets/physics/test_mflux/char_galileo_v2.png`, `data/assets/physics/test_mflux/char_aristotle_v2.png`.

Detailed implementation rules for typography, palette, texture, layout, and motion are documented in:

- `../../operations/21_storybook_visual_style_guide.md`

---

## SECTION 1 — CHARACTER ROSTER

Every historical figure with a visual role in the series. Sorted by frequency and production priority.

---

### TIER 1 — CRITICAL PRIORITY (appears across 5+ topic modules)

---

**ISAAC NEWTON**
- Modules: 01_newtons_laws, 02_conservation_laws, 03_gravity_orbits, 04_oscillations_waves, 05_lagrangian_mechanics, 06_hamiltonian_mechanics, 07_rigid_body_dynamics, 08_central_force_problems, 02_electromagnetism (all subtopics), 03_thermodynamics (kinetic theory, stat mech), 04_quantum_mechanics (matrix mechanics)
- Script appearances: 73 of 150 scripts (49% of the series)
- Era: Late 17th–early 18th century English natural philosopher
- Visual description: Pale face, long curled silver-white wig reaching shoulders, dark navy frock coat with large brass buttons, white cravat at neck, serious focused expression
- Status: ✅ Approved reference — `data/assets/physics/test_mflux/char_newton_v2.png`
- Priority: **CRITICAL — generate first, reuse everywhere**

---

**ALBERT EINSTEIN**
- Modules: 01_newtons_laws, 02_conservation_laws, 03_gravity_orbits, 04_oscillations_waves, 05_lagrangian, 06_hamiltonian, 08_central_force, 02_electromagnetism (Maxwell eqs, EM waves, special relativity), 03_thermodynamics (classical, kinetic, stat mech quantum), 04_quantum_mechanics (origins, wave mechanics)
- Script appearances: 39 of 150 scripts
- Era: Early 20th century, ~1905–1930
- Visual description: Wild tousled white hair (iconic), bushy white mustache, warm brown eyes, rumpled academic jacket or gray sweater
- Status: Character concept exists; finalize to board-book style
- Priority: **CRITICAL**

---

**JAMES CLERK MAXWELL**
- Modules: 05_lagrangian, 06_hamiltonian, 02_electromagnetism (all 5 subtopics), 03_thermodynamics (kinetic theory, stat mech quantum), 04_quantum_mechanics (origins, wave mechanics)
- Script appearances: 37 of 150 scripts
- Era: Victorian, mid–late 19th century Scottish
- Visual description: Thick full dark beard, wavy dark hair, Victorian academic frock coat (dark), white shirt with wing collar, calm thoughtful expression
- Status: Not yet generated
- Priority: **CRITICAL**

---

**WILLIAM ROWAN HAMILTON**
- Modules: 01_newtons_laws, 02_conservation_laws, 05_lagrangian, 06_hamiltonian_mechanics (featured), 03_thermodynamics (phase transitions), 04_quantum_mechanics (origins, wave, matrix, angular momentum)
- Script appearances: 30 of 150 scripts
- Era: Early–mid 19th century Irish mathematician
- Visual description: Long wavy dark-brown hair slightly disheveled, 19th century academic frock coat with wide lapels, white stock collar, dreamer's expression
- Status: Not yet generated
- Priority: **CRITICAL**

| Rudolf Clausius | 03_thermodynamics/01 | Mid–late 19th cent. German | Victorian academic, beard |

## SECTION 3 — BACKGROUND SCENES

### 3A — HISTORICAL / ARCHITECTURAL SETTINGS

| Background | Module Topics | Visual Description |
|-----------|--------------|---------------------|
| **Ancient Greek agora / courtyard** | 01_newtons_laws ep1, 03_gravity_orbits | White Ionic columns flanking a warm terracotta floor, single olive tree, pale blue sky, golden warm light |
| **Galileo's ramp laboratory (Padua/Pisa)** | 01_newtons_laws ep2, 03_gravity_orbits ep2 | Stone-wall interior, arched window with warm daylight, wooden ramp on stone floor |
| **Newton's study (Trinity College Cambridge)** | 01_newtons_laws ep3–7 | Dark wood paneling, diamond-pane window, candles on desk, stacked leather books |
| **Tycho Brahe's Uraniborg observatory** | 03_gravity_orbits ep2 | Stone circular building, large brass quadrant instruments, starry night sky behind |
| **Kepler's study / Prague (~1600)** | 03_gravity_orbits ep3–4 | Cluttered Renaissance study, planetary data tables, small globe |
| **Göttingen University (early 20th cent.)** | 02_conservation_laws ep7, 05_lagrangian ep7 | Neoclassical German building, cobblestones, green lawn |
| **Victorian laboratory (Faraday / Joule era)** | 02_electromagnetism, 03_thermodynamics | Gas-lit stone room, wooden workbench, glass jars and wire coils, tall windows |
| **Early 20th century physics institute** | 04_quantum_mechanics | Large blackboard, simple wooden chairs, 1920s German academic interior |
| **Franklin's Philadelphia study** | 02_electrostatics ep1 | Colonial-era room, candle light, wooden desk, papers |

---

### 3B — ABSTRACT / PHYSICS BACKGROUNDS

| Background | Module Topics | Visual Description |
|-----------|--------------|---------------------|
| **Deep space (black + stars)** | 03_gravity_orbits, 08_central_force, 02_conservation_laws | Black background, scattered white and pale yellow star dots |
| **Solar system overhead view** | 03_gravity_orbits | Sun at center, elliptical orbit curves, colored planet circles |
| **Phase space portrait (abstract)** | 06_hamiltonian | Grid background, colored curved trajectories (ellipses, spirals, separatrix) |
| **Quantum wave function (abstract)** | 04_wave_mechanics | Wavy colored sinusoidal/Gaussian curves on coordinate axis |
| **Blackbody glow spectrum** | 04_QM ep1, 03_thermo/stat_mech_quantum | Dark background, glowing gradient red-to-white in bell-curve shape |
| **EM field lines (abstract)** | 02_electromagnetism | Curved lines radiating from point charge, or parallel between plates |
| **Warm pastel solid** | All series | Cream, pale blue, pale lavender, or soft yellow — flat color only |
| **Dark solid / near-black** | All series | Deep navy or near-black for equation overlay scenes |

## SECTION 4 — DIAGRAMS AND VISUAL ELEMENTS

### 4A — PHYSICS DIAGRAMS (Manim-generated — no image generation needed)

| Diagram | Topics | Description |
|---------|--------|-------------|
| Free body diagram | 01_newtons_laws | Object with labeled force arrows (W, N, F, T, friction) |
| Force / velocity arrow | All classical mechanics | Colored arrow with label |
| Inclined plane force decomposition | 01_newtons_laws ep7, ep8 | Gravity split into parallel/perpendicular components |
| Momentum collision (before/after) | 02_conservation_laws | Ball A and B with labeled momentum arrows |
| Energy bar chart (KE + PE) | 02_conservation_laws | Stacked bar chart showing KE + PE = constant |
| Potential energy curve U(x) | 02_conservation_laws ep6, 06_hamiltonian | Parabola, 1/r gravity well, double-well potential |
| Angular momentum L diagram | 02_conservation_laws ep5 | L = r × p cross product, right-hand rule |
| Orbital diagram (Kepler ellipse) | 03_gravity_orbits, 08_central_force | Ellipse, Sun at focus, equal-area sectors shaded |
| Retrograde motion loop | 03_gravity_orbits ep1 | Mars path tracing a loop against background stars |
| Ptolemaic epicycle | 03_gravity_orbits ep1 | Deferent, epicycle, equant labeled |
| Kepler's 3rd law data table | 03_gravity_orbits ep4 | T, a, T²/a³ columns |
| Cavendish balance labeled | 03_gravity_orbits ep6 | Torsion wire, rod, small and large masses |
| Pendulum force diagram | 04_oscillations ep2 | L, m, θ labeled; mg decomposed |
| SHO phase portrait ellipse | 06_hamiltonian ep2–3 | x-p ellipse on phase space axes |
| Phase space trajectories | 06_hamiltonian ep3 | Multiple trajectories with separatrix |
| Liouville phase fluid | 06_hamiltonian ep3 | Cloud of points flowing without volume change |
| Inertia tensor 3×3 matrix | 07_rigid_body ep2 | Symmetric matrix with diagonal principal moments |
| Euler's equations (3 coupled) | 07_rigid_body ep3 | Three equations in a box |
| Gyroscope precession diagram | 07_rigid_body ep4 | Spin axis, torque, precession vectors |
| Coriolis force diagram | 07_rigid_body ep5 | Earth globe, Ω vector, v vector, F_Cor arrow |
| Foucault pendulum rotation | 07_rigid_body ep5 | Top-view clock face showing plane rotation |
| Electric field lines (point charge) | 02_electrostatics ep3 | Radial lines outward from + charge |
| Gauss's law surface | 02_electrostatics ep4 | Sphere surrounding a charge, area element dA |
| Magnetic field around wire | 02_magnetostatics ep3 | Circular field lines around wire cross-section |
| Biot-Savart setup | 02_magnetostatics ep3 | Current element, r vector, dB direction |
| Ampere's loop | 02_magnetostatics ep4 | Rectangular or circular Amperian loop around wire |
| Faraday induction / flux | 03_maxwell ep1–2 | Loop in changing B field, flux lines threading through |
| Maxwell's 4 equations | 03_maxwell ep4 | All four equations displayed simultaneously |
| EM wave oscillation | 03_maxwell ep5, 04_em_waves ep1 | E and B sinusoidal waves, perpendicular, propagating |
| Polarization diagram | 04_em_waves ep2 | E-field oscillation plane, polarizer filter |
| Snell's law refraction | 04_em_waves ep3 | Light ray bending at interface, θ₁ and θ₂ |
| Diffraction pattern | 04_em_waves ep4 | Central max, side maxima on a screen |
| Interference fringes | 04_em_waves ep5 | Double slit, constructive/destructive fringe pattern |
| Spacetime diagram | 05_special_relativity | ct axis, x axis, world lines |
| Lorentz transformation equations | 05_special_relativity ep3 | t' and x' equations boxed |
| Maxwell-Boltzmann speed distribution | 03_kinetic_theory ep2 | Bell-curve f(v) vs v with v_mp, v_avg, v_rms |
| Boltzmann entropy S = k ln W | 03_stat_mech ep2 | Large clean equation on screen |
| Partition function Z | 03_stat_mech ep5 | Z = Σ e^(-βE) equation |
| Fermi-Dirac vs Bose-Einstein | 03_stat_mech_quantum ep2–3 | Two distribution curves on f(E) vs E axes |
| Planck blackbody spectrum | 04_QM ep1, 03_thermo/quantum | u(ν,T) vs ν curve, Rayleigh-Jeans vs Planck |
| Compton scattering diagram | 04_QM ep6 | Photon hitting electron, deflected photon + recoiling electron |
| Bohr model hydrogen | 04_QM ep4 | Nucleus + discrete circular orbits |
| Wave function ψ(x) probability | 04_wave_mechanics ep2 | |ψ|² plotted, shaded area |
| Particle-in-a-box energy levels | 04_wave_mechanics ep3 | Horizontal levels in a well, n=1,2,3 |
| Quantum harmonic oscillator levels | 04_wave_mechanics ep4 | Parabola with equally spaced levels |
| Hydrogen orbital shapes | 04_wave_mechanics ep5 | s, p, d probability clouds (simplified) |
| Quantum tunneling | 04_wave_mechanics ep6 | Wave function decaying through a potential barrier |
| Dirac notation | 04_matrix_mechanics | Bra-ket notation, commutator brackets |
| Uncertainty principle statement | 04_matrix_mechanics ep5 | Δx · Δp ≥ ℏ/2 boxed |
| Stern-Gerlach splitting | 04_angular_momentum ep3 | Beam splitting into two spots on detector |
| Spin up/down arrows | 04_angular_momentum ep4 | ↑ and ↓ arrows for spin states |
| Symmetry-conservation table | 02_conservation_laws ep7, 05_lagrangian ep7 | 3-row table: symmetry → conservation law |
| Timeline graphic | All series | Horizontal timeline with named figures and dates |
| Hierarchy tree diagram | 01_newtons_laws ep12 | Newton at base, branches to relativity, QM, field theory |

### 4B — REUSABLE UI / OVERLAY ELEMENTS

| Element | Use | Notes |
|---------|-----|-------|
| Equation box (bordered) | All series | Dark-outlined rectangle surrounding key equations |
| Quote card | Multiple topics | Dark bg, serif font, white text, for historical quotes |
| Timeline dot markers | Multiple topics | Small circles with date labels on a horizontal line |
| Chapter title card | All series | Episode title + topic name on solid color background |
| Before/After comparison frame | Conservation laws, collisions | Two-panel split |
| Force arrow (long, red) | Mechanics | ✅ Already generated in library |
| Force arrow (short, orange) | Mechanics (deceleration) | ✅ Already generated in library |
| Question mark bubble | Multiple topics | Large "?" for mystery moments |
| Warning / boundary card | End of many episodes | "Where this breaks down" styled card |

## SECTION 7 — SERIES-WIDE SUMMARY

### Asset Count by Category

| Category | Unique Assets Needed | Already Have | Still to Generate |
|----------|---------------------|--------------|-------------------|
| Tier 1–2 Characters (critical/high) | 17 | 3 (Newton, Galileo, Aristotle) | 14 |
| Tier 3 Characters (medium priority) | 12 | 1 (Einstein concept) | 11 |
| Tier 4 Characters (low / text-card acceptable) | ~35 | 0 | ~15 (selectively) |
| Mechanics Objects | 26 | ~12 (existing library) | ~14 |
| EM Objects | 15 | 0 | 15 |
| Thermo / Quantum Objects | 16 | 0 | 16 |
| Instruments and Tools | 7 | 2 | 5 |
| Historical Scene Backgrounds | 9 | 2 (Greek courtyard, basic space) | 7 |
| Abstract Backgrounds | 8 | 3 (space, dark, gradient) | 5 |
| Manim-generated Diagrams | ~55 | n/a (code-generated, not illustrated) | 0 |
| UI / Overlay Elements | 9 | 3 | 6 |

**Approximate net new images to generate: ~88**
**Already in library: ~17 approved assets**
**Total illustrated assets across series: ~105**

---

### Reuse Statistics (Key Insights)

| Asset | % of Scripts Where It Appears |
|-------|-------------------------------|
| Newton character | 49% (73/150) |
| Einstein character | 26% (39/150) |
| Maxwell character | 25% (37/150) |
| Hamilton character | 20% (30/150) |
| Red ball + ramp | ~40% of Module 1 |
| Dark solid background (equations) | ~65% of all screen time |
| Earth globe | ~10% across all modules |
| Deep space background | ~10% across all modules |

Generating the top 17 characters covers the vast majority of all character screen appearances.
Manim handles ~60–70% of total screen time (equations, text, diagrams) — illustrated assets cover the rest.
Each module's first 2–3 episodes introduce nearly all the objects that module reuses.

---

### Character Production Batches

**Batch 1 — Generate now (Module 1 production start):**
Newton ✅ | Galileo ✅ | Aristotle ✅ | Euler | Lagrange | Emmy Noether | Kepler | Hamilton | Descartes | Leibniz | Huygens

**Batch 2 — Generate for Module 2 (Electromagnetism):**
Maxwell | Faraday | Franklin | Gilbert | Oersted | Lorentz | Hertz | Ampère | Lenz

**Batch 3 — Generate for Modules 3–4 (Thermo + Quantum):**
Planck | Boltzmann | Einstein ✅ | Carnot | Joule | Bohr | Heisenberg | Schrödinger | Dirac | Born | Bose | Fermi

**Batch 4 — Lower priority (generate selectively or use text cards):**
Rumford | Maupertuis | d'Alembert | Cavendish | Tycho Brahe | Ptolemy | Copernicus | Clausius | Kelvin | Gibbs | Landau | Ising | Sommerfeld | de Broglie | Compton | Pauli | Stern | Gerlach | Rutherford | Thales | Wallis | Wren | Richmann | Hooke | Foucault | Coriolis | Biot | Savart
